entries.gameplayFunction: Forest edge, concealment, and biome identity.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Canopy shape must remain readable at gameplay height.
entries.materialNotes: Bark, leaf mass, branch rhythm, age variation.
entries.colorNotes: Biome-led seasonal control, not saturated fantasy greens.
entries.scaleNotes: Large world silhouettes with clear collision expectations.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Edge readability matters more than botanical excess.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs biome-specific LODs and collision classes.

entries.gameplayFunction: Terrain edge support and micro-cover shaping.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Distinct clusters for stone, stump, and debris shapes.
entries.materialNotes: Rock strata, weathering, cut wood grain.
entries.scaleNotes: Small to medium set pieces for terrain breakup.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Avoid prop clutter that obscures unit selection.
entries.unityImportNotes: Useful as nav blockers and sightline shapers.

entries.gameplayFunction: Ownership, House identity, and command visibility.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Broad cloth read and standard-top silhouette must survive zoom.
entries.materialNotes: Cloth, timber, metal finials, rope ties.
entries.scaleNotes: Varies from wall banners to field standards.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Must help recognition without flooding the battlefield.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs wind animation options and attach-point standards.

entries.gameplayFunction: Settlement dressing, economy-site readability, and logistics support.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Civilian cart, supply cart, and pile types must remain distinct.
entries.materialNotes: Timber, rope, sacks, barrels, cut stone, timber stacks.
entries.colorNotes: Mostly material-led with small House cloth markers.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Keep readable from above; do not overfill market or camp areas.
entries.unityImportNotes: Useful for map dressing and economy-site state swaps.

entries.gameplayFunction: Battle consequence and siege aftermath read.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Low-profile remains that do not hide living units.
entries.materialNotes: Broken timber, bent iron, cloth tears, limited corpse dressing.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Support consequence without turning battlefields into cluttered gore fields.
entries.unityImportNotes: Likely spawned conditionally and cleaned aggressively.

entries.gameplayFunction: Faith presence, boundary marking, and watch logic.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Small sacred or warning silhouettes readable from path level and RTS height.
entries.materialNotes: Stone, timber, bone-free unless covenant-specific, fire bowls, cloth strips.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Keep covenant language specific and materially credible.
entries.unityImportNotes: Useful for sacred sites, keeps, and route warnings.

entries.gameplayFunction: Route shaping, camp readability, and crossing support.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Fence rhythm, tent massing, and bridge span must read from above.
entries.materialNotes: Timber, rope, canvas, planks, piles, dock hardware.
entries.colorNotes: Low saturation, with House marks only where owned.
entries.scaleNotes: Small fencing to medium crossing structures.
entries.generationPromptNotes: These should strengthen route readability, not decorate it away.
entries.unityImportNotes: Bridge and dock variants support future river and naval lanes.

entries.gameplayFunction: Historical world layering and infrastructural storytelling.
entries.visualRole: Memory of prior civilization and reclaimed-space logic.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Broken arches, columns, water channels, fallen lintels.
entries.materialNotes: Aged stone, plaster traces, moss, repair patches.
entries.colorNotes: Desaturated mineral tones with climate influence.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Old-world depth without turning maps into archaeology exhibits.
entries.unityImportNotes: Important for frost ruins, oldcrest lanes, and world hubs.
